title: Considering the current market regime for the model forward testing .
author: marius_o_h
selftext: I built a model which predicts the direction of the stock for the next 5, 10, 20, 30 and 40 days. The backtesting looks very promising and 2 weeks ago I started the forward testing of the model. However the current market regime  looks very different  , the stocks are heavily correlated with the oil movements that's why I would invalidate the prediction from the last 2 weeks in order to not pollute the forward testing results. What is your opinion about that?

comments.author: BackTesting-Queen
comments.body: In my experience, it's crucial to test your model in various market conditions, including those that are not favorable. While it's tempting to invalidate the predictions from the last two weeks due to the current market regime, I would advise against it. This period could provide valuable insights into how your model performs when stocks are heavily correlated with oil movements. Remember, a robust model should be able to adapt to different market scenarios. Instead of discarding this data, consider it as a stress test for your model. It's all part of the journey to refining your trading strategy.
